meta.note: Public testdata images and synthetic fixtures only. Per-stage numbers are p50 from topo_stage_timing; the decode sweep is the synthetic-board criterion bench.

images.label: Sparse chessboard
images.note: A small ~1 MP board: corner detection is ~75% of the whole pipeline, and DiskFit roughly doubles it. The owned grid build is a thin slice.

images.label: Dense board
images.note: A 12 MP board with ~20k corners: the regime flips. The owned grid build is now 27.5 ms, dominated by ordering + recovery (both driven by the per-corner local-homography solve in the precision gate).

end_to_end.desc: Full chessboard pipeline (corner detection + grid build, ring-fit), per public frame.

sweep.desc: PuzzleBoard master-edge-code decode on a synthetic board, by board size (cells per side). The full master sweep searches all 501x501 origins x 8 D4 transforms.

roadmap.title: Corner-detect config levers
roadmap.evidence: ~75% on a 1 MP board; 14 ms at 3 MP; DiskFit ~2x
roadmap.approach: keep RingFit default; optional downscale / ROI for large frames

roadmap.title: PuzzleBoard master-origin scan
roadmap.evidence: fixed O(8x501^2) scan, dominant on small/mid boards
roadmap.approach: CRT-separable collapse to O(8x501) — byte-exact, deterministic

roadmap.title: ChArUco board-match decode
roadmap.evidence: decode can exceed corner detection on dense boards

roadmap.title: Per-corner local-H solve (precision gate)
roadmap.evidence: ordering + recovery own the dense-board grid build
roadmap.approach: memoize local-H bases across grow iterations (high risk: determinism + precision gate)

roadmap.title: Orientation-free positions-only grid path
roadmap.evidence: ~8.5x the oriented path on synthetic grids
